--- title: "Handout 8: Relational Data and Joins" format: html df-print: kable --- ::: {.cell} ::: :::callout-warning In progress! For class, I am imagining this would be rendered as a .docx and need some editing to be filled out by students. ::: We have 7 datasets that relate to movies from IMDb's database, as shown in the graphic below. ![](_images/08-imdb_relational.png) 1. Just looking at the graphic, draw lines between the variables that you think are the keys between the different datasets. ::: {.cell} ::: 2. ::: {.cell} ::: :::columns :::{.column width=45%} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} directors ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| id|first_name |last_name | |------:|:------------|:---------| | 15092|Ethan |Coen | | 15093|Joel |Coen | | 15901|Francis Ford |Coppola | | 15906|Sofia |Coppola | | 48115|George |Lucas | | 842813|Greta |Gerwig |
::: ::: ::: :::{.column width=5%} ::: :::{.column width=45%} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} movies ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| id|name | year| rank| |------:|:--------------------------|----:|----:| | 109093|Fargo | 1996| 8.2| | 130128|Godfather, The | 1972| 9.0| | 192017|Little Mermaid, The | 1989| 7.3| | 194874|Lost in Translation | 2003| 8.0| | 237431|O Brother, Where Art Thou? | 2000| 7.8| | 238072|Ocean's Eleven | 2001| 7.5| | 300229|Shrek | 2001| 8.1| | 313459|Star Wars | 1977| 8.8|
::: ::: ::: ::: 3. Fill in the columns below as if we completed this join. Leave elements blank that would be missing! ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} movies_directors |> left_join(directors, by = join_by(director_id == id)) |> left_join(movies, by = join_by(movie_id == id)) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display}
